relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: After covenant breaks, new stone and a wooden ark are prepared.
The same words will be written again and given a place to remain.
Moses participates in repair with his own hands.
The Ten Words return from the fire.
Renewed covenant is placed inside an ark, not left exposed to another fall.
Aaron dies, and Eleazar carries the priesthood forward.
The journey passes into a land of streams.
Levi is separated to carry, serve, stand, and bless.
Its inheritance is vocation and dependence upon YHWH.
Moses' intercession is heard again.
The people are told to rise and continue the journey.
Reverence, walking, love, service, heart, and soul gather into one response.
Commandment is named as being for the people's good.
Heaven and earth belong to YHWH.
Within that vastness, ancestral love chooses a particular people.
The heart itself must lose its protective hardness.
The great Divine shows no partiality and accepts no bribe.
Greatness becomes justice for orphan and widow and food and clothing for the immigrant.
Israel must love the sojourner because Israel remembers being one.
Reverence becomes service, clinging, and truthful allegiance.
Praise rises from remembered liberation.
Seventy descendants become a multitude like stars.

contemplative_note: Deuteronomy 10 gives the cleanest measure of Divine greatness in the whole section: no partiality, no bribe, justice for orphan and widow, and food, clothing, and love for the immigrant. Any spirituality that fails this test has missed the chapter.
